frameworks.name: rate-distortion theory
frameworks.one_line: optimal information compression under capacity limits shapes latent representations

frameworks.name: Beta Variational Autoencoder (β-VAE)
frameworks.one_line: generative model used to instantiate and probe rate-distortion trade-offs

frameworks.name: internal models of the world
frameworks.one_line: cognitive framing motivating why organisms must compress representations

core_question: Which specific distortions of latent representations emerge when generative models face rate-distortion trade-offs?
key_assumptions: organisms rely on resource-limited internal models that must compress information
compression manifests as distortion of latent representations
rate-distortion theory specifies optimal compression given capacity, frequency, and utility
key_findings: three primary distortions emerge: prototypization, specialization, and orthogonalization
distortions arise as signatures of adaptation to capacity, data imbalance, and task demands
multiple distortions can coexist within a single latent space
interpretations: distortion signatures index how generative models adapt to resource and task constraints
latent-space geometry varies systematically across models with different rate-distortion constraints
